title: Should we move these bad wells? Or have better way to use them.
content:
Testing CV, we found some type wells extremely bad on RSME, like 20+. And other wells’ RSME are good and similar. I wonder if top team delete bad training data for a better fitting model? Worry about overfitting, we haven't use it yet.

Will the distribution of the test data be similar to that of the training data?

comments.content:
Testing CV, we found some type wells extremely bad on RSME, like 20+.

what do you mean by bad type wells? - bad predictions on horizontal?
